methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $250M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Palantir
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $400B+

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Twelve Labs, a Series A AI & Software company founded by Jae Lee and Aiden Lee, is valued at $250M post-money on $57M raised to date. The peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private) in the AI & Software sector at similar stage, including Palantir, Liquid AI, Field AI, OpenAI, and xAI.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to next stage, implies $500M valuation. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a $2.4B implied value, though the peer range is notably wide ($2.0B to $400.0B), heavily influenced by Palantir's public market valuation outlier.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: the peer set contains significant heterogeneity (Palantir's $400B public valuation versus Liquid AI and Field AI near $2.0B–$2.4B), and stage-progression methodology alone implies a more conservative $500M floor. Key risks to valuation include the representativeness of private comps for an early-stage frontier-tech company and the applicability of public trading multiples to Series A-stage entities. Further diligence on revenue trajectory and unit economics would narrow the range.
